steps.title: Track the process described
steps.content: The passage explains Cumberland Mountain, a pumped-storage hydropower facility. It describes water being pumped uphill when demand is low, stored as gravitational potential energy, and released downhill to spin turbines when demand peaks.

steps.title: Reject purposes it does not have
steps.content: Choice A would require comparing two methods of energy generation, but the text focuses on one facility. Choice C calls the technology new and benefit-focused, while the passage mainly describes operation. Choice D is a recommendation to utilities, but the passage does not urge anyone to build facilities.

steps.title: Choose the mechanism purpose
steps.content: Since the passage walks through how this specific water-battery stores and releases energy, its main purpose is explanatory: it gives the basics of how a specific energy technology works. The answer is B.
